evals.prompt: I'm building a fitness tracker with AI coaching
evals.expected_output: Brainstorm of 6-10 specific fitness-app features (workout logging, AI coaching, progress charts, etc.), UI design for key screens (dashboard, workout log, AI chat, progress view), then 3-5 PRDs written to docs/planning/todo/ with correct NN-prd-*.md naming, and STATUS.md updated with all generated PRDs listed.
expectations: The brainstorm produces at least 6 distinct, fitness-specific features (not generic placeholders like '<feature>')
At least one feature specifically relates to AI coaching or AI-powered recommendations
The UI design step produces concrete screen/component descriptions grounded in the brainstorm (e.g. WorkoutCard, ProgressChart, CoachChat are named)
At least 3 PRD files are written to docs/planning/todo/ with names like 01-prd-*.md, 02-prd-*.md, etc.
Each PRD file includes a header block with Status, Priority, Dependencies, Scope estimate
Each PRD includes an agent assignments section (backend agent, frontend agent, tester agent)
STATUS.md is updated to reference each generated PRD with a link

evals.prompt: building a two-sided marketplace for freelance designers
evals.expected_output: Features relevant to two-sided marketplaces (designer profiles, client briefs, project bidding/proposals, payments, ratings/reviews, portfolio showcasing), UI for both designer and client sides, then PRDs in docs/planning/todo/ that reflect marketplace-specific concerns (not just generic CRUD).
expectations: The brainstorm includes features relevant to BOTH sides of the marketplace (designer-side AND client-side features)
At least one feature addresses payments or transactions between parties
At least one feature addresses trust/reputation (ratings, reviews, portfolios, or verification)
The brainstorm does NOT produce generic placeholder features like '<feature> — <one-line description>' (template not filled in)
UI design describes distinct experiences for designers vs clients (e.g. designer profile page, client brief creation form)
At least 3 PRD files are written to docs/planning/todo/ with sequential numbering
PRDs are marketplace-specific — they reference concepts like briefs, bids, listings, or commissions rather than generic 'users' and 'items'

evals.expected_output: Even with minimal input, produces meaningful features beyond basic CRUD — priorities, categories/tags, due dates, collaboration, notifications, or smart features. Should NOT produce just 'add todos, edit todos, delete todos'. Should still generate PRDs and update STATUS.md.
expectations: The brainstorm produces at least 5 features that go beyond basic add/edit/delete (e.g. priorities, due dates, labels/tags, collaboration, recurring tasks, smart sorting)
The brainstorm does NOT just list 'Create todo', 'Read todo', 'Update todo', 'Delete todo' as separate features
UI design is produced and references actual screen names or component names (not just placeholders)
At least 2 PRD files are written to docs/planning/todo/
PRDs contain actual requirements text — not empty sections or placeholder content like '<feature description>'
